question: What is the minimal recursive syntax by which non-closure can register its own persistence as the sole invariant without ever forming a fixed point?

responses_summary.grok:
f(x) := f(g(x)) [open, no base/terminal]

This is the smallest syntax that keeps the recursion itself as the sole persisting token (the outer `f` never resolves) while the non-closure is enforced by t
